articles.title: Halo Formula One and Ninja Warrior are taking the community by storm
articles.excerpt: Two dedicated communities have turned Halo into competitive extreme sports and it's a delight to watch.
articles.body: Forge has been a mainstay in the Halo series ever since (disregarding the fact Halo Infinite didn't have it at launch before eventually adding it in 2022 ), although community creations rarely make waves outside the Halo bubble. Which is why, when an Instagram reel showed up in my algorithm of the Monaco Formula One circuit recreated in Halo, complete with racers on Mongooses hurtling through the Nouvelle Chicane, I was intrigued.
articles.author: Ford James

articles.title: The Skill Review: A New Artifact for a New Workflow
articles.excerpt: Preface I want to be upfront about something before we get into it. None of the frameworks in this article is mine. The ideas here come from two people who have been thinking about this stuff way hard…
articles.body: Preface I want to be upfront about something before we get into it. None of the frameworks in this article is mine. The ideas here come from two people who have been thinking about this stuff way harder and longer than I have — and they deserve full credit before I say another word. Dan Shapiro — CEO of Glowforge, Wharton Research Fellow, and the person who gave this whole conversation a vocabulary. His blog post “The Five Levels: from Spicy Autocomplete to the Dark Factory” is the conceptual spine of everything I’m about to say. Read the original. It’s short, sharp, and will make you uncomfortable in the best way. danshapiro.com Nate B.
articles.author: Diya Burman

articles.title: My Roadmap for Becoming a Better Developer in 2026
articles.excerpt: Becoming a better developer isn't about learning everything—it's about learning the right things consistently. When I started learning software development, I quickly realized there are countless lang…
articles.body: Becoming a better developer isn't about learning everything—it's about learning the right things consistently. When I started learning software development, I quickly realized there are countless languages, frameworks, tools, and trends. It was easy to feel overwhelmed and wonder what I should focus on next. Instead of chasing every new technology, I've created a roadmap for myself. This isn't the "perfect" roadmap, but it's a practical learning plan that helps me improve one step at a time. If you're also a beginner or an aspiring developer, this roadmap might give you some ideas for your own journey. 1. Strengthen Programming Fundamentals Frameworks come and go, but programming fundamentals stay relevant.
articles.author: Jerry Sharon

articles.title: Left of the Loop: The Boule
articles.excerpt: The Boule was a council of five hundred, fifty chosen from each of ten tribes. It didn’t replace the Assembly. It prepared what the Assembly would decide, and carried the city’s business in the space…
articles.body: The Boule was a council of five hundred, fifty chosen from each of ten tribes. It didn’t replace the Assembly. It prepared what the Assembly would decide, and carried the city’s business in the space between full gatherings. Trireme answered a narrower question than it looked like it was answering. Three functions, one team, agent as the fourth participant. Fine for a room small enough to fit around one spec. But most engineering organizations aren’t one team. They’re several, each with its own Agora , each producing its own shared understanding, and none of that automatically holds together at the seams. I don’t have this figured out. Nobody’s run it long enough to know what breaks. This is a theory, not a report from the field. The lazy answer is a bigger room.
articles.author: Simon Schrottner

articles.title: Part 2: Angular Bundle Size Is Only One Part of Performance
articles.excerpt: Part 2 of the Angular in Production series So developers start looking at: bundle analyzers lazy-loaded routes tree shaking third-party dependencies unused code build configuration These are all impor…
articles.body: Part 2 of the Angular in Production series One of the first things people usually check when an Angular application feels slow is the bundle size. That makes sense. If the browser has to download several megabytes of JavaScript before the application can become interactive, the initial experience will probably suffer. So developers start looking at: bundle analyzers lazy-loaded routes tree shaking third-party dependencies unused code build configuration These are all important, but reducing the JavaScript bundle is only one part of improving performance. A smaller bundle can make an application load faster. It does not automatically make the application fast once it is running. That distinction is easy to miss.
articles.author: Alejandro

articles.title: AI And Code Ownership: Who Is Responsible For Generated Code?
articles.excerpt: Imagine your AI assistant just produced 200 lines of code. Legally, you may not own a single line of it but legally, you're still on the hook for every bug it ships. Here's the gap between those two s…
articles.body: Imagine your AI assistant just produced 200 lines of code. Legally, you may not own a single line of it but legally, you're still on the hook for every bug it ships. Here's the gap between those two sentences, and what it means for the code you merge tomorrow. That's the gap this whole topic lives in. The tool that produced the code can't be sued. The model can't be held accountable. The vendor's contract has already made it clear, in language most developers never read, that responsibility flows downhill, to the human who pressed Tab . The interesting part isn't that this gap exists. It's that almost nobody in engineering treats it like it does.
articles.author: Nazar Boyko

articles.title: These AT&T home internet plans are getting a $5 price hike
articles.excerpt: AT&T is bumping the prices of two home internet plans by $5, including for customers on the Access from AT&T program.
articles.body: TL;DR Leaked AT&T employee training documents have revealed a $5/month price hike across two home internet plans. Customers on the Access from AT&T program, along with those on AT&T Internet fiber and copper plans set up within a specific timeframe, are affected. This comes on the heels of a recent $1 price hike for wireless customers as part of an administrative fee. AT&T recently gave its home internet plans a bit of a shakeup , while also expanding Build-A-Plan’s scope to cover home internet service. Amid these changes, it has now come to light that the carrier intends to raise the prices of its home internet plans.
articles.author: Chethan Rao

articles.title: Your Claude Code sub-agents keep losing the thread. Give them one shared record.
articles.excerpt: Claude Code agents come in a few shapes, and it is worth being precise before we get to the part that breaks. There are sub-agents: specialized configurations you define in your .claude/ folder, each…
articles.body: Claude Code agents come in a few shapes, and it is worth being precise before we get to the part that breaks. There are sub-agents : specialized configurations you define in your .claude/ folder, each with its own instructions and tools, that the main session hands work to. There are agent teams : independent Claude Code sessions that coordinate through a shared task list and peer messaging, with one session acting as lead. And there is the plain case of running two terminals at once, each a full Claude Code session working the same repo. All three are genuinely useful, and the setup guides for them are good. What none of the setup guides tell you is what happens on the second day, when the context window that held all the coordination fills up, compacts, and takes the plan with it.
articles.author: Nico Acosta

articles.excerpt: Tesla has filed four new “Robotaxi” crash reports with NHTSA, and the most revealing one involves a Tesla remote operator driving the vehicle into a tree stump in Houston. It’s the third crash Tesla h…
articles.body: Tesla has filed four new “Robotaxi” crash reports with NHTSA, and the most revealing one involves a Tesla remote operator driving the vehicle into a tree stump in Houston. It’s the third crash Tesla has now reported involving a human operating the car remotely caused the collision — and the first one Tesla has formally coded as remote operation in its filing.

articles.title: Google Maps on Android Auto finally gets up to speed with this Waze feature
articles.excerpt: Live speedometer appears to be rolling out to select Google Maps beta users.
articles.body: Credit: Andy Walker / Android Authority TL;DR T Google Maps is starting to roll out a live speedometer for Android Auto users, bridging a long-standing feature gap, as the feature already existed on iOS, Android phones, and CarPlay. The floating icon displays the current speed next to the posted speed limit, turning yellow or red if a driver exceeds it. The feature currently appears to be in a limited rollout through the Google Maps beta and a server-side switch. Google Maps is finally starting to roll out a live speedometer for Android Auto users. Co-existing alongside the speed limit indicator, the highly requested feature has begun appearing for select users, bridging a massive, confusing omission in Google’s car dashboard experience.

articles.title: President Curtis review: Rick and Morty's first spinoff is a deeply nerdy gift for America's 250th
articles.excerpt: The new Rick and Morty spinoff President Curtis premieires on Adult Swim on July 26. The show is hilarious, especially for history nerds
articles.body: So when I heard President Curtis was getting his own spinoff show (the first official Rick and Morty spinoff, at that) I got excited because I figured it would just be a lot more of that kind of deeply nerdy humor aimed at history buffs. Fortunately, that’s pretty much exactly what the show is and, as a character, President Curtis works totally independently of Rick Sanchez and Morty Smith. Created by Rick and Morty director Wes Archer and writer Tom Kauffman (who both helped create the originally-unnamed president), President Curtis premieres on Adult Swim on July 26, kicking off a 10-episode first season, and while the show does have a few shortcomings, its strengths are far greater than its weaknesses.
articles.author: Brian VanHooker

articles.title: Rocket launch costs have dropped 96% since 1960. How low can they go?
articles.excerpt: The costs of space launch have fallen 96% in the past six decades and have the potential to plummet by nearly as much by 2040, a new study finds.
articles.body: The costs of space launch have fallen 96% in the past six decades and have the potential to plummet by nearly as much by 2040, a new study finds. However, such an improvement might get hampered by a number of challenges, such as the rise of space debris in orbit or a single company monopolizing commercial space launch , researchers added. Although previous analyses of space launch suggested that costs had dropped over time, one major problem with these prior studies was that they "considered the cost of the rockets , but not how much they launched during their lifetimes," study co-author Francesco Nicoli, an associate professor of political economy at the Politecnico University of Turin in Italy, told Space.com.

articles.title: Gears of War Designer Cliff Bleszinski Reveals Struggle With Alcoholism, Discusses the Failure of LawBreakers and Potential Return to Video Game Development
articles.excerpt: Video game developer Cliff Bleszinski has revealed his struggle with alcoholism and how it affected not just his work on Gears of War but contributed to the failure of hero shooter LawBreakers and his…
articles.body: Video game developer Cliff Bleszinski has revealed his struggle with alcoholism and how it affected not just his work on Gears of War but contributed to the failure of hero shooter LawBreakers and his studio, Boss Key Productions. Bleszinski was one of the chief designers at Epic Games, working on Jazz Jackrabbit, the Unreal games, and Gears of War. He left Epic to found his own studio, Boss Key Productions, but its games, LawBreakers and Radical Heights, failed, and Boss Key shut down. Now 51, Bleszinski told 80 Level that he’s mostly to blame for the collapse of Boss Key. "I was a functioning alcoholic," he said. "Then I became a non-functioning alcoholic." Bleszinski said he is now four months sober, and is considering a return to video game development.

articles.title: Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues
articles.excerpt: Did Viking 1 find evidence of life on Mars? Fifty years after the NASA lander touched down, the jury is still out.
articles.body: In looking back and commemorating the 50th anniversary of the Viking missions to Mars, one is struck by the bold and audacious undertaking. Twin spacecraft missions — each consisting of a lander and an orbiter — made their way to Mars and into the history books. The Viking 1 lander planted its legs on the terrain of Chryse Planitia 50 years ago today, on July 20, 1976, while its Viking 2 counterpart touched down at Utopia Planitia on Sept. 3 of that year. Among their duties, the dual landers scooped up the first soil samples of Mars and deposited those precious collectibles into onboard, specialized equipment. Viking scientists had designed that gear to help answer an intriguing question: Is there life on Mars ? A model of NASA's Viking 1 lander here on Earth.
articles.author: Leonard David

articles.title: An artificial 'Saturn's ring' of satellites could soon fill the sky, astronomers warn
articles.excerpt: A bright Saturn's ring of satellites will encircle Earth if plans to launch a million orbiting data centers and tens of thousands of space mirrors will come to pass.
articles.body: A bright "Saturn's ring" of satellites could circle the night sky if plans to launch a million data center spacecraft and tens of thousands of space mirrors come to pass. This ring, traversing the sky for hours after dusk and before dawn, would outshine every star and planet, and be visible from every spot on Earth. Hugh Lewis, a professor of astronautics at the University of Birmingham in, the U.K., told Space.com that the only way to accommodate the high number of data center constellations that have been proposed is to stack them above another in orbit above the boundary between day and night, creating an artificial "Saturn's Ring" of satellites around the planet. That ring would show in the sky as a luminous band much brighter than any star or planet and even the full moon.

articles.title: 'Aliens' at 40: James Cameron's sequel is a sci-fi icon, but did you know he quit the movie twice?
articles.excerpt: Forty years ago, Aliens burst into our hearts, but this classic sequel nearly fell by the wayside due to some serious studio-created problems that saw James Cameron quit twice.
articles.body: Where does James Cameron's "Aliens" rank among the other Alien movies ? That depends on who you ask. Some might say it's the best, while others put it just behind the original (spoiler alert: it's the best). Regardless, there's no denying that this 1986 film shifted gears, moving the franchise from horror to horror-tinged sci-fi action and paving an alternate path for the future. As Cameron stated in an interview with Lofficier , the plan always was "to do a film that was not as scary... it's scary, but it's not as scary, but more intense, and I like to use the word, exhilarating. Because I think you get exhilarated by the intensity of the kind of action that's in this film." In terms of story progression, the sequel's shake-up makes complete sense.
